Admission Date:  1988-09-07              Discharge Date:   2018-09-26

Date of Birth:  1975-03-28             Sex:   F

Service: CARDIOTHORACIC

Allergies:
Penicillins / Motrin / Naprosyn

Attending:Dennis
Chief Complaint:
Shortness of breath

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
1979-03-29 Mitral valve replacement(27mm [**Company 1543**] Mosaic Porcine)
and single vessel coronary artery bypass grafting utilizing the
left internal mammary artery to left anterior descending artery.


History of Present Illness:
This is a 79 year old female with known history of coronary
artery disease,  mitral regurgitation and paroxysmal atrial
fibrillation. She presented to Healthsouth Rehabilitation Hospital Of Tallahassee complaining of acute
shortness of breath awakening her from sleep. Chest x-ray at
that time was consistent with pulmonary edema. EKG showed normal
sinus rhythm. Symptoms did improve with diuresis. Subsequent
cardiac catheterization revealed a 60% in-stent stenosis in the
proximal LAD stent and a patent RCA stent. The circumflex was
noted to have only a 50% lesion. Left ventriculography confirmed
4+ mitral regurgitation and estimated LV function around 60%.
Based upon the above results, she was transferred to the Molokai General Hospital
for cardiac surgical intervention. Prior to transfer, she was
started on antibiotics for a urinary tract infection.

Past Medical History:
Congestive heart failure, Coronary artery disease - History of
Coronary Stents(LAD & RCA) in 2008, Mitral regurgitation,
Paroxsymal Atrial fibrillation/flutter, Type II Diabetes
mellitus; Hypertension, Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease,
Hypercholesterolemia, Pulmonary Hypertension; History of GI
bleed and Peptic Ulcer Disease, History of Bradycardia - s/p
Pacemaker Implantation, Anemia, Hypothyroidism

Social History:
Married, lives alone. She does not smoke or drink alcohol.

Family History:
Denies history of premature coronary artery disease

Physical Exam:
Admission Vitals: T 98.3, BP 106/50, HR 60, RR 20, SAT 100% on
2L
General: elderly female in no acute distress
HEENT: oropharynx benign,
Neck: supple, no JVD, carotids 2+ without bruits
Heart: regular rate, normal s1s2, s3 noted, 3/6 systolic murmur
left lower sternal border
Lungs: bibasilar rales
Abdomen: soft, nontender, normoactive bowel sounds
Ext: warm, no edema, no varicosities
Pulses: decreased distally
Neuro: nonfocal

Echocardiographic Measurements
Results  Measurements  Normal Range
Left Atrium - Long Axis Dimension:  *5.6 cm  <= 4.0 cm
Left Ventricle - Inferolateral Thickness:  0.9 cm  0.6 - 1.1 cm

Left Ventricle - Diastolic Dimension:  5.5 cm  <= 5.6 cm
Left Ventricle - Ejection Fraction:  50% to 55%  >= 55%
Aorta - Sinus Level:  2.1 cm  <= 3.6 cm
Aortic Valve - Peak Gradient:  5 mm Hg  < 20 mm Hg
Aortic Valve - Mean Gradient:  3 mm Hg

Findings
LEFT ATRIUM: Moderate LA enlargement. No spontaneous echo
contrast in the body of the LA. No spontaneous echo contrast or
thrombus in the body of the [**Name Prefix (Prefixes) **] [**Last Name (Prefixes) **] LAA. Good (>20 cm/s) LAA
ejection velocity. No thrombus in the LAA.

RIGHT ATRIUM/INTERATRIAL SEPTUM: Normal RA size. No spontaneous
echo contrast in the body of the RA. A catheter or pacing wire
is seen in the RA. No ASD by 2D or color Doppler.

LEFT VENTRICLE: Normal LV wall thickness. Normal LV cavity size.
Low normal LVEF. [Intrinsic LV systolic function likely
depressed given the severity of valvular regurgitation.]

RIGHT VENTRICLE: Normal RV wall thickness. Normal RV chamber
size. Normal RV systolic function. [Intrinsic RV systolic
function likely more depressed given the severity of TR].

AORTA: Normal aortic diameter at the sinus level. Simple
atheroma in aortic root. Focal calcifications in aortic root.
Normal ascending aorta diameter. Simple atheroma in ascending
aorta. Focal calcifications in ascending aorta. Normal aortic
arch diameter. Simple atheroma in aortic arch. Normal descending
aorta diameter. There are complex (>4mm) atheroma in the
descending thoracic aorta.

AORTIC VALVE: Normal aortic valve leaflets (3). Mildly thickened
aortic valve leaflets. No AS. Trace AR.

MITRAL VALVE: Moderately thickened mitral valve leaflets. No
mass or vegetation on mitral valve. Moderate mitral annular
calcification. Moderate thickening of mitral valve chordae. No
MS. Moderate to severe (3+) MR. Raymond vena contracta is >=0.7cm

TRICUSPID VALVE: Mildly thickened tricuspid valve leaflets. No
mass or vegetation on tricuspid valve. Normal tricuspid valve
supporting structures. No TS. Moderate [2+] TR. Moderate PA
systolic hypertension.

PULMONIC VALVE/PULMONARY ARTERY: Normal pulmonic valve leaflets
with physiologic PR.

PERICARDIUM: No pericardial effusion.

GENERAL COMMENTS: A TEE was performed in the location listed
above. I certify I was present in compliance with HCFA
regulations. No TEE related complications. The TEE probe was
passed with assistance from the anesthesioology staff using a
laryngoscope. The patient was under general anesthesia
throughout the procedure.

REGIONAL LEFT VENTRICULAR WALL MOTION:





     N = Normal, H = Hypokinetic, A = Akinetic, D = Dyskinetic
Conclusions
PRE BYPASS:
1. The left atrium is moderately dilated. No spontaneous echo
contrast is seen in the body of the left atrium. No spontaneous
echo contrast or thrombus is seen in the body of the left atrium
or left atrial appendage. No thrombus is seen in the left atrial
appendage. No spontaneous echo contrast is seen in the body of
the right atrium. No atrial septal defect is seen by 2D or color
Doppler.
2. Left ventricular wall thicknesses are normal. The left
ventricular cavity size is normal. Overall left ventricular
systolic function is low normal (LVEF 50-55%). [Intrinsic left
ventricular systolic function is likely more depressed given the
severity of valvular regurgitation.] Right ventricular chamber
size is normal. Right ventricular systolic function is normal.
[Intrinsic right ventricular systolic function is likely more
depressed given the severity of tricuspid regurgitation.]
3. There are simple atheroma in the aortic root. There are
simple atheroma in the ascending aorta. There are simple
atheroma in the aortic arch. There are complex (>4mm) atheroma
in the descending thoracic aorta. The aortic valve leaflets (3)
appear structurally normal with good leaflet excursion.
4. The aortic valve leaflets are mildly thickened. There is no
aortic valve stenosis. Trace aortic regurgitation is seen.
5. The mitral valve leaflets are moderately thickened. No mass
or vegetation is seen on the mitral valve. There is moderate
thickening of the mitral valve chordae. Moderate to severe (3+)
mitral regurgitation is seen. The mitral regurgitation vena
contracta is >=0.7cm.
6. The tricuspid valve leaflets are mildly thickened. Moderate
[2+] tricuspid regurgitation is seen. There is moderate
pulmonary artery systolic hypertension. There is no pericardial
effusion.
There is flow reversal in the hepatic vein. Small left sided
pleural effusion.
POST BYPASS:
Pt. is AV paced, on phenylephrine and epinephrine drip.
Preserved biventricular systolic function
MV - well seated bioprosthetic valve with trace of paravavular
leak.
Normal aortic contour post decanulation.
Mild TR, improved from pre bypass.
I certify that I was present for this procedure in compliance
with HCFA regulations.

Electronically signed by Allison Salazar, MD, Interpreting
physician

FINAL REPORT


INDICATION: 79-year-old woman with mitral regurgitation, CAD.

COMPARISON: Chest x-ray dated 2018-07-27.

FINDINGS: PA and lateral views of the chest were obtained. The
patient is
again noted to be status post CABG and mitral valve replacement.
A right-
sided dual lead pacer device is in stable position. Bilateral
pleural
effusions and lower lobe airspace opacities are unchanged. Mild
cardiomegaly
is unchanged. There is cephalization of the pulmonary
vasculature without
edema. No pneumothorax is present.

THE STUDY AND THE REPORT WERE REVIEWED BY THE STAFF RADIOLOGIST.

DR. Allison Kim

DR. [**First Name11 (Name Pattern1) **] [**Initial (NamePattern1) **] Lewis


Approved: WED 1973-09-20 9:42 AM
Procedure Date:2008-04-24





Brief Hospital Course:
Mrs. Vance was admitted directly to the cardiac surgical
service and underwent routine preoperative evaluation. Workup
was unremarkable and she was cleared for surgery. The following
day, she underwent a mitral valve replacement and coronary
artery bypass grafting by Dr. Sellers. For surgical details,
please see separate dictated operative note. After the
operation, she was brought to the CSRU for invasive monitoring.
Within 24 hours, she awoke neurologically intact and was
extubated without incident. She experienced rapid atrial
fibrillation/flutter. Episodes were broken by rapid atrial
pacing via epicardial wires. Amiodarone was avoided in setting
of Dofetilide therapy. The EP service was consulted and
recommended to increase Dofetilide and monitor QTc intervals by
serial ECG. Intravenous beta blockade was used on a prn basis
for rate control. Given persistent atrial tachycardias, her
pacemaker was reprogrammed for some atrial lead undersensing.
The pacemaker overall, had good lead function. She tolerated
beta blockade and Dofetilide. QTc intervals remained stable. By
postoperative day three, she was being anticoagulated and
eventually converted to a normal sinus rhythm. She maintained
stable hemodynamics and transferred to the SDU for further care
and recovery. She continued to increase her activity level and
was cleared for discharge to rehab on POD#11.


Medications on Admission:
Atenolol 25 Healthsouth Rehabilitation Hospital Of Tallahassee, Dofetilide 500 mcg Healthsouth Rehabilitation Hospital Of Tallahassee, Lisinopril 10 qd, Lasix
40 qd, Glyburide 5 Healthsouth Rehabilitation Hospital Of Tallahassee, Glucophage 500 qam and 1000 qpm, Folate,
Aspirin 81 qd, Lipitor 20 qd

Discharge Disposition:

Facility:
Petersen Petersen hospital

Discharge Diagnosis:
Congestive heart failure, Coronary artery disease, Mitral
regurgitation - s/p coronary artery bypass grafing and mitral
valve replacement; Paroxsymal Atrial fibrillation/flutter; Type
II Diabetes mellitus; Hypertension; COPD; Hypercholesterolemia;

Disease; History of Coronary Stents(LAD & RCA) in 2008; History
of Bradycardia - s/p Pacemaker Implantation, Anemia,
Hypothyroidism, Preoperative Urinary Tract Infection


Discharge Condition:
Good


Discharge Instructions:
Patient may shower, no baths. No creams, lotions or ointments to
incisions. No driving for at least one month. No lifting more
than 10 lbs for at least 10 weeks from the date of surgery.
Monitor wounds for signs of infection. Please call with any
concerns or questions.

Followup Instructions:
Dr. Sellers in 06-25 weeks, call for appt
Local PCP, Williams. Benjamin Jason Meadows in 04-19 weeks, call for appt
Local cardiologist, Dr. James Jones in 04-19 weeks, call for appt
